name: Community Development Corporation Of Long Island Dba Community Development Long Island
description_short: Long Island nonprofit CDFI providing housing counseling, financial coaching, and affordable housing programs to low-income individuals and families since 1969.
description_long:
Community Development Long Island (CDLI) is a regional nonprofit organization founded in 1969 by government, business, and civic leaders to address affordable housing demand on Long Island. As a Certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) and chartered NeighborWorks America member, CDLI operates as a full-service collection of community development entities: CDLI Parent Corporation, CDCLI Funding Corporation, and CDCLI Housing Development Corporation. The organization serves over 25,000 individuals annually across Nassau and Suffolk counties, focusing on Long Island's most marginalized residents.

CDLI offers a comprehensive suite of housing and financial empowerment services rather than traditional consumer lending. Their primary offerings include the Housing Choice Voucher Program (currently paused for new issuances as of February 2024), homeownership preparation through education and mortgage-readiness coaching, financial coaching and resources, landlord-tenant assistance programs, and developer partnerships for affordable housing development. The organization also provides CDFI lending options and rental opportunity connections to help residents find affordable housing.

What distinguishes CDLI is its integrated approach combining housing solutions with financial empowerment and community development. As the only NeighborWorks organization on Long Island, it operates with tri-sector support and serves as a "go-to organization for all things home" with explicit focus on fair housing and integration goals. The organization employs over 115 community-centered professionals and meets high standards of fiscal integrity and service performance.

CDLI functions primarily as a community development nonprofit and financial counselor rather than a traditional alternative finance company. While they offer CDFI lending, their core value lies in education, housing vouchers, homeownership coaching, and community partnership. The voucher program pause (effective February 2024) significantly limits current rental assistance availability. Those seeking emergency cash loans or payday alternatives would be better served elsewhere; CDLI is best suited for individuals seeking long-term housing stability, financial education, and community development support.
pros: Only NeighborWorks organization on Long Island, bringing national expertise with local focus
Comprehensive mission addressing housing AND economic aspirations, not just lending
Serves 25,000+ individuals annually with 115+ dedicated community professionals
CDFI certification ensures lending compliance and community benefit focus
Multilingual support (English, Spanish, Hindi, Chinese) for diverse Long Island communities
Free financial coaching and resources available to qualified individuals
Founded in 1969 with 55+ years of track record serving Long Island residents
cons: Housing Choice Voucher Program paused for new issuances as of February 2024 with no end date specified
Limited emergency lending—focused on long-term housing solutions rather than quick cash access
Geographic limitation to Long Island (Nassau and Suffolk counties only)
Heavy emphasis on homeownership preparation may not serve renters seeking immediate housing
No traditional payday loan or short-term emergency cash products available
best_for: Long Island residents seeking homeownership education and mortgage-readiness coaching
Individuals needing financial literacy and long-term economic stability planning
Low-income families wanting to understand fair housing rights and rental options
Community development partners and property developers working on affordable housing
diagnosis: CDLI is best for Long Island residents seeking long-term housing stability, financial education, and community support rather than emergency cash or traditional lending. Primary caveat: the Housing Choice Voucher Program (major assistance vehicle) is currently paused with no specified restart date, and the organization serves primarily nonprofit/community development functions rather than consumer lending.
services: Housing Choice Voucher Program (currently paused for new issuances)
Financial coaching and financial fitness education
Homeowner resources and home improvement education
Tenant resources and landlord assistance programs
CDFI lending options for community members
Rental opportunity matching and affordable housing search
Property developer partnerships for affordable housing development
Fair housing education and integration advocacy
Community partnership and collaborative development services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
